meta.name: RUE2 Grammar Tree - Minimal Viable Model
meta.description: Phase 1 canonical definition of the Grammar Tree. Extracted and normalized from the reference documents in _reference/. This is the proposed source of truth going forward.
meta.source: Based on Tree_Model_Of_Language_Proficiency.md, Tree Roots by Level — Master Grammar Index.md, and Grammar Tree by Root.md

roots.name: Tap Root
roots.description: Word-class recognition (N/V/Adj/Adv/Prep/Det/Pron) + default SVO architecture. Foundational layer that everything else depends on.

roots.name: Verb Phrase
roots.description: Tense, aspect, modality, voice. The largest and most complex root.

roots.name: Noun Phrase
roots.description: Determiners, pronouns, quantifiers, comparison within the noun phrase.

roots.name: Sentence Syntax
roots.description: Word order, questions, negation, inversion, clefts, basic agreement at sentence level.

roots.name: Clause Linking
roots.description: Subordination, relatives, conditionals, reported speech, coordination, discourse markers.

roots.name: Verb Complementation
roots.description: Patterns after verbs: gerund vs infinitive, transitivity, causatives, etc.

roots.name: Prepositions & Particles
roots.description: Preposition system, phrasal verbs, dependent prepositions, particles.

families.name: Present Perfect
families.description: Present perfect simple and continuous, including since/for distinctions.

families.name: Conditionals
families.description: Zero, First, Second, Third, Mixed conditionals and variants.

families.name: Articles & Determiners
families.description: a/an/the, zero article, generic reference, institutional uses.

families.name: Modal Verbs
families.description: Core modals, speculation (present & past), past forms of modals.

families.name: Phrasal Verbs
families.description: Separable/inseparable phrasal verbs and multi-word verbs.

families.name: Prepositions
families.description: Time, place, movement, dependent, and complex prepositions.

families.name: Reported Speech
families.description: Statement and question reporting, tense backshift, reporting verbs.

design_principles: Every content unit should eventually declare exactly one primary root_id.
Families are the main grouping concept for UI (roughly maps to current 'topics').
Some families legitimately span multiple roots (e.g. conditionals, reported speech, passives). These get secondary_root tags for diagnostics.
This file is intentionally minimal for Phase 1. We can add rule/lexical face distinctions, full root_id lists per family, and richer metadata later.
